Bind Sight  

Description

1: Bind Sight

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 15 Skill: Divination
Casting Time: 4 Recast Time: 10
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 10000
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Single
Spell Type: Beneficial Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: Your sight is bound.
Cast on other: Soandso 's eyes gleam and then go dark.
Effect Fades: Your binding ends.

Game Description

Covers your face in magical strands, allowing you to see through your target's eyes.

Items with this effect

  • Clairvoyant Orb
  • Eyepatch of Scrying
  • Spell: Bind Sight
  • The Reflecting Pools of Tanaan

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Bind Sight
ClassLevel
RNG17
WIZ16
ENC8
Expansion:

Duration:
2.6 mins @L8 to 14.0 mins @L65
  • Comments (15)
  • Sold By (19)
  • Screenshots

Post Comment
fun and usefull spell.
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2007 at 1:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591828020915
882 posts
This spell is like duct tape.has a thousand and 1 uses,all of them pretty cool.Havnt tried it but i bet you could get some pretty good screenshots from the mobs point of view on raids.
▲ Page top
So useful
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2005 at 4:05 AM Rating: Excellent
KroniKmigraine
3 posts
Okay, I will spill the beans on the greatest use for this spell.

Yes, I use it for fun. Its hilarious on a raid, to bind sight all the way to the last mob, even when raid is forming. People wonder if you are cheating :)

But for practical uses, as a ranger, I have had people ask me, "Whoa, how did you pull that?" or "How did you break that camp?" The secret? This spell. Lets say you're going into a room. You can see the first mob, but that's it. You target them, step back a bit, and cast Bind Sight. Then you can look around, target, and harmony every other mob in the room. Then simply retarget the main one, hit it, and step back. I've harmonied my way all the way to Venril Sathir, solo, without killing a single mob using this method.

Use this tool to its advantage, and it will take you everywhere. I pity the rangers I talk to who don't even know this exists.

Thanks
Alan
▲ Page top
Possible use?
QuoteReply
# Mar 03 2004 at 4:53 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344491541
View User Forum
54 posts
can this spell be used on a tank as he goes into a place away from caster. Can the caster then cast spells from far away place just by looking through tanks eyes?
▲ Page top
RE: Possible use?
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2004 at 10:00 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593966118024
319 posts
yes - but normal range rules apply. The caster can however use the tanks vision to target mobs he normally could not see for pacification for example
▲ Page top
RE: Possible use?
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2005 at 5:14 PM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
This is true but misleading. The range on this spell is Huge. Large enough to cover most zones, and I myself have never received the out of range message when using it.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2003 at 1:19 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591789974715
Scholar
258 posts
Sold by the first clockwork merchant to the right of Juline Urncaller. Location +1100 -983.
▲ Page top
zoning while binded
QuoteReply
# May 21 2003 at 7:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789013364
12 posts
does the binded sight stick through zoning?
▲ Page top
RE: zoning while binded
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2004 at 8:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759514467
51 posts
This spell will not stay after a zoning, also it has a limited range (like 500 feets seems) and it's duration is restricted by your stamina pool. Fun part is that your target got NO CLUE you casted it :oP

Edited, Tue Jan 6 08:28:15 2004
▲ Page top
RE: zoning while binded
QuoteReply
# May 23 2003 at 6:24 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257735806
13 posts
No. Your sight will return to normal if you or your target leaves the zone where you bound your sight.
▲ Page top
a worthwhile spell
QuoteReply
# May 14 2003 at 10:58 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592257735806
13 posts
I have had a lot of fun with this spell! Friends with the spell bind my sight, while I bind to theirs. Emoting each other to see what we look like to others, like looking in the mirror. Or, bind sight to an enemy to get a feel for what it's like to get its butt kicked by you.

On the more practical side of this spell, I have found it most benificial and use it quite often. You can use it a number of ways to your advantage as a reconosance tool. My personal favorite is to bind to someone who is about to go into a place I have not been, or bind to a player of a higher season than myself that is going where I dare not go! Another way to use as recon would be to set up players in your group at lookout locatoins in a zone with that rare spawn you've been hunting for weeks (for those of you without tracking) while you bind to your competition's sight, incase they see it first. Tell your man closest to the mob to get the jump on it before it falls prey to another camp! Yes, a ruthless hunter am I. Although that would be more along the lines of hunting strategy than spell usage. Just one of the ways I have kept my edge over other players after the same thing.

It's also a great way to find the patrol paths of mobs you don't care to get to close to yet, or patrols through a group of mobs. Just be sure you're in a relatively safe place before you use it for any significant length of time. It has a range that will stretch from one side to the other of most zones, so you can sit wherever and med., hide, fish (until you catch something), etc, while you learn patrol paterns or just see the sights of the wondering wildlife.

I'm sure there are ways to use this spell that would suit anyone. I would highly recomend shelling out the few plat. it costs to buy the spell, even if you don't use it in your daily casting scheme. I hope I have instilled some inSIGHT to one of the most fun spells in the game. <8-P

I have'nt had a chance to try the spell on pets, so I can't comment on the possabilties there (or if it would work on a pet or not). Rangers dont get many pets. I'd sure like to see some info there.

▲ Page top
Pets, and Soloing
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2003 at 10:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787161089
Sage
64 posts
As a Shammy soloing in Dreadlands I got tons of
good use from the Shaman's version of this spell known as Vision. Sometimes an add would come along and if it was a close fight, I would beat the first mob down until he ran and then let him run off.. After I had dispatched the add, I could use the key command for 'last target' to target the original mob (who was sometimes halfway across the zone by the time I caught up) and then bind sight to it.

It was a fun little game to use the landmarks to determine which way the mob was heading since I didn't have any tracking abilities.

I also was able to park my pet and use him as a lookout to make sure the camp was clear before I started to pull a mob from far away. It's a great, mana cheap spelly imho and some folks dont even know it exists.

Casters Realm -- Bind Sight.

Casters Realm is pretty good for much of the information you need on spells and such. Another good site you may one day need is devoted primarily to spell research -- Mythiran Tower.

I hope you find what you need. Best of luck.
